tiny_practice.action: Take 30 seconds to silently affirm: 'God's righteousness is unchanging, even when I am unfaithful'

two_step.reflect_question: How does understanding universal human sinfulness change my view of judgment and grace?

options.text: I see myself as equally in need of God's mercy
options.response: Practice daily humility and gratitude for unmerited grace

options.text: I recognize no one is righteous by their own merit
options.response: Extend compassion to others, knowing all need redemption

options.text: God's justice requires a perfect solution beyond human effort
options.response: Deepen trust in Christ's redemptive work

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a scale with human actions on one side and God's grace on the other, showing how grace outweighs our limitations

reevaluation.question: How has this chapter shifted your understanding of righteousness?
options: I now see righteousness as a gift, not an achievement
I understand God's justice is more complex than I previously thought
I recognize the universal need for redemption
I'm more aware of God's consistent character

three_choice.question: Which core theological insight resonates most deeply?

choices.expansion: Divine reliability exists independent of human inconsistency
All humans share a fundamental brokenness before divine standards
Salvation emerges from divine mercy, not human performance

socratic_nudge.question: If human unfaithfulness actually highlights God's faithfulness, can failure itself become a pathway to understanding grace?

moral_slider.question: Where do you locate righteousness: in personal effort or divine intervention?

reevaluation.question: How has your understanding of redemption shifted?

thematic_summaries.title: Universal Sinfulness
thematic_summaries.summary: All humanity, both Jews and Gentiles, are under the condemnation of sin. No one is righteous by their own merit, and everyone stands equally guilty before God's divine judgment.

thematic_summaries.title: Justification by Faith
thematic_summaries.summary: Righteousness is not achieved through works of the law, but through faith in Jesus Christ. God provides salvation as a free gift, demonstrating His justice and mercy simultaneously.

reevaluation.question: How does human sinfulness reveal God's righteousness?
options: God's perfect justice is demonstrated through humanity's moral failure
Divine grace becomes more magnificent when contrasted with human weakness
The depth of God's redemptive plan is revealed through universal human brokenness
Human sin provides an opportunity for God to display transformative mercy
